chapter.title: Grafana
chapter.para: In this chapter, we will see how to use Grafana to visualize the data we have in our database.

section.title: Setting up the Visualization Dashboard
section.para: We can use Grafana, an open-source technology, to create a business intelligence dashboard. This will allow different users to set up their own queries and visualizations, or easily slice through data in a visual way for non-technical users.
Start by setting up Grafana on your system:

listitem.programlisting: brew update brew install grafana brew services start grafana

# Note These are instructions for Grafana Enterprise Edition (via APT repository), # which they recommend. It includes all the Open Source features and can also use # Enterprise features if you have a License.
# Setup Grafana Keys sudo apt-get install -y apt-transport-https sudo apt-get install -y software-properties-common wget wget -q -O - https://packages.grafana.com/gpg.key | sudo apt-key add -
# Add repository for stable releases echo "deb https://packages.grafana.com/enterprise/deb stable main" | sudo tee -a /etc/apt/sources.list.d/grafana.list
# Install Grafana sudo apt-get update sudo apt-get install grafana-enterprise

listitem.para: Use the Windows installer available at the Grafana website.

section.title: Configuration
section.para:
Once Grafana is installed, you need to start your Grafana server:
sudo /bin/systemctl start grafana-server We can now sign in to Grafana by going to http://localhost:3000/. Set up a new account if needed. Additional instructions to login can be found here following the build your first dashboard instructions.
Next, we add a data source for Grafana to interact with. In this case, we can follow the Grafana instructions for adding a data source and search for PostgreSQL as the data source.
The workshop is using the following settings to connect to the postgres server.

section.title: Queries
section.para: Now that Grafana is connected to your database, you can start creating queries to visualize your data.

section.title: Dashboards
section.para: Finally, you can create dashboards to visualize your data in a more user-friendly way. You can find more information on how to create dashboards on the Grafana documentation.

section.title: Conclusion
section.para: In this chapter, we have seen how to use Grafana to visualize the data we have in our database. Grafana is a powerful tool that allows you to create beautiful and interactive dashboards to monitor your data in real-time.
